english: me, I could not reconcile myself to it'; *blo skyél-ba* (blo skyel ba) W., *k̇el-ba* (khel ba) C., čel-ba (chel ba) Cs. (?), to rely, to depend upon, blo gél-ba (blo gel ba) to hope Sch. (the correct spelling as yet doubtful); blos gtóṅ-ba (blos gtong ba) to give up, resign entirely, to risk, venture, e.g. raṅ-gi srog (rang gi srog) Glr., Mil., blo spáṅ-ba (blo spang ba), id. Mil.; *tse-‿di lụ-táṅ* (tshe 'di lu tang) monk C.; *ó-cag blos ma toṅs-par* ('o cag blos ma tongs par) as she was so much attached to us Mil.; raṅ-blos ma toṅs-pa (rang blos ma tongs pa) a man attached to himself, in love with himself; blo gtód-pa (blo gtod pa) Schr to trust, confide (cf. compounds); dé-las blo zlóg-pa (de las blo zlog pa) Thgy., to subtract, to draw off, divert, dissuade from; blo bríd-pa (blo brid pa) to deceive, impose upon, cheat Glr. (bló-yi bdag (blo yi bdag) 'conscience' Sch., acc. to Schr. not an authenticated expression). Comp. blo-kóg-čé (blo khog che) confident, courageous, intrepid, undaunted. -- blo-grós (blo gros) sense, intellect, understanding; blo-grós-kyi śés-bya (blo gros kyi shes bya) what is to be discerned by the understanding; blo-grós daṅ ldan-pa (blo gros dang ldan pa), blo-grós-čan (blo gros can) sensible, judicious (of persons), blo-grós čén-po (blo gros chen po) C. of much sense, of an excellent understanding, čuṅ-nu (chung nu) C., źán-pa (zhan pa) Mil. of little understanding; blo-gros-méd (blo gros med) unintelligent, injudicious; blo-gros-rgyal-po (blo gros rgyal po) n. of a medicinal plant, = smug-čuṅ (smug chung) Wdn. -- bló-čan (blo can) having mind, sense; byís-pai blo-čan (byis pa'i blo can) having the mind or sense of a child, thinking like a child Cs.; having a mind, v. above mčód-pa byéd-pai bló-čan de (mchod pa byed pa'i blo can de). -- blo-nyés (blo nyes) ill-meaning, malicious Glr. -- blo-gtád (blo gtad), blo-gdeṅ (blo gdeng) hope, confidence, assurance, bdág-gi blo-gdeṅ sú-la ča (bdag gi blo gdeng su la cha) in whom am I to place my confidence. -- blo-gdeṅ čós-la byéd-pa (blo gdeng chos la byed pa) Glr.; W.: *lor-tád* (lor tad) or *lo-dáṅ čó-če* (lo dang cho che), *kyél-če(s)* (kyel che(s)), c. la. -- blo-rtóg (blo rtog) prob. = blo-grós (blo gros), blo-rtóg ta-dád-pa (blo rtog tha dad pa) Pth. people of different mental abilities. -- blo-stóbs (blo stobs) 1. C., W. courage. 2. W., generosity, magnanimity, or perh. also equanimity, self-command, e.g. if a person remains kind and forbearing towards disobedient servants. -- blo-tábs (blo thabs) counsel, expedient, blo-tábs tsól-ba (blo thabs tshol ba) Ma. -- blo-bde (blo bde) cheerful, happy. -- blo-‿dód (blo 'dod) covetous, greedy. -- bló-sna (blo sna) 1. bló-sna maṅ-ba (blo sna mang ba) Glr. was explained by our Lama: having manifold thoughts, being restless, flighty, giddy. 2. W. disposition, turn of mind, *ló-na ríṅ-mo* (lo na ring mo) slowness, irresolution, also longsuffering, *lo-na túṅ-se* (lo na tung se) resoluteness, determination, promptness, both also adj.: slow, irresolute, and: resolute, determined etc. -- blo-méd (blo med) injudicious, foolish Cs. (Dzl. 24, 18 makes no sense, there being prob. an error in the text. The translation of Sch. seems to be a mere conjecture). -- blo-bzáṅ (blo bzang) 'sound sense', col. *lob-zaṅ* (lob zang), a very common name of persons. -- blo-śéd (blo shed) Sch. 'memory, intellectual power'. -- blo-séms (blo sems) mind, soul, heart, blo-sems-bdé (blo sems bde) = blo-bdé (blo bde) Mil. -- blo-bsám (blo bsam) intellect; W.: *lo-sám méd-k̇an* (lo sam med khan) foolish, one not knowing what he is about. II. frq. incorr. for glo (glo).

I. vb. to be able = pód-pa (pod pa); k̇yod mi ló-na (khyod mi lo na) if you cannot; *di mi lo* ('di mi lo) that you cannot (dare not) do, prob. only W. vulg. -- II. sbst. = blo (blo), frq. used by Mil. for the sake of the rhythm.

= gló-bur (glo bur) sudden, suddenly; k̇yed dá-ltar-gyi dád-pa bló-bur yin (khyed da ltar gyi dad pa blo bur yin) thy present faith is new, but just sprung up in thee Mil.; mí-spyod bló-bur-du gyur (mi spyod blo bur du gyur) the conduct of men suddenly changes Ma.
for lón-mo (lon mo), bones or knuckles used as dice Mil.
1. Lex. = gros (gros), blón ‿débs-pa (blon 'debs pa) to give advice, to counsel; Cs.: to make arrangements. -- 2. v. the following.

officer (prop. counsellor), any magisterial officer of higher rank; blón-po daṅ ‿baṅs (blon po dang 'bangs) commanding and obeying, higher officers and subalterns Glr.; more particularly minister (of state); blon(-po) čen(-po) (blon po chen po) Glr., blón-po bká-la gtógs-pa (blon po bka' la gtogs pa) Glr., more commonly bka-blón(-po) (bka' blon po), high officer of state, minister, governor; k̇rims-blón (khrims blon) minister of justice, officer of justice; rgyal-blón (rgyal blon) king and minister, also = council, privy-council, Glr.; čós-blon (chos blon) 1. (opp. to bdúd-blon (bdud blon)) an orthodox, faithful minister etc. 2. čos-blon čén-po (chos blon chen po) minister of public worship Glr.; rj́e-blón (rje blon) the same as rgyal-blón (rgyal blon) Glr. -- spyi-blon (spyi blon) chief officer Cs. -- pyi naṅ bar gsúm-gyi blón-po (phyi nang bar gsum gyi blon po) Glr., lit outer, inner, middle
